commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainer review should accept the internal UI compatibility boundary and reconcile the overlapping history PR; there is no narrow automated repair finding from this pass.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff stays within Gateway/message routing and adds handling to keep sensitive source-reply media out of durable transcript mirrors. (sha=c663d2ab000a54c8263b53d3990a454ce9e8fa4c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes message-tool-only source replies for internal OpenClaw UI clients into the active Gateway/TUI run while preserving the legacy `webchat` tool-result channel, adding source-reply display/transcript helpers, media metadata preservation, tests, and TUI docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main has the stale-route preference path and lacks the broader internal chat surface routing helper, while the PR body includes a base negative control plus live after-fix TUI/Gateway proof.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review should accept the internal UI compatibility boundary and reconcile the overlapping history PR; there is no narrow automated repair finding from this pass.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body: ## Summary - Fix message-tool source replies from internal OpenClaw chat surfaces so TUI/WebChat receive the visible reply in the active run instead of inheriting a stale external delivery route. - Keep the legacy tool-result/action channel as `webchat` for compatibility, and use `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` as the internal sink marker. - Keep external channels such as Telegram, Slack, and Discord as separate deliverable channels; this does not make their conversations visible in WebChat/TUI. - Split the source-reply display/mirror path into focused gateway helpers, add coverage for current-run routing, and remove PR-introduced dead exports (`isTuiClient`, `GatewayCurrentSessionClientId`, `GatewayInternalChatSurfaceClientId`). ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #38957 ## Design Boundary This PR intentionally does not create a new public channel, config key, CLI flag, provider policy, or external delivery path. `internal-ui` is an internal sink marker for message-tool source replies on OpenClaw-owned chat surfaces such as TUI, WebChat, Control UI, and native UI mode. The compatibility boundary is that `MessageActionRunResult.channel` and the model-facing result payload `channel` stay `webchat`. Existing agents, workflows, downstream parsers, and source copies that key on the historical message-tool `channel` value continue to see the old route label. New code should read `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` when it needs to know that the visible source reply was delivered into the OpenClaw-owned internal UI sink. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `message_tool_only` source replies render in the active TUI/current-run chat even when the session has a stale external delivery route, and the tool result keeps `channel: "webchat"` while identifying the visible sink with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`. Real environment tested: Live local dev host on exact PR head `c663d2ab000a54c8263b53d3990a454ce9e8fa4c` (`OpenClaw 2026.5.21 (c663d2a)`), fresh isolated OpenClaw home at `/tmp/openclaw-tui-proof-c663-MUKIQj`, foreground Gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:42865`, throwaway loopback token auth, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, `gateway.controlUi.enabled: false`, bundled plugins from this worktree, live `openai/gpt-5.5` resolved to `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`. Each proof session was pre-seeded with stale external delivery metadata `lastChannel: "telegram"` and a redacted Telegram target before sending the TUI prompt. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash PROOF_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-tui-proof-c663-MUKIQj PORT=42865 : "Fresh isolated home configured with throwaway token, portable per-agent auth, messages.visibleReplies=message_tool, disabled Control UI assets, skipped channels, and a stale Telegram route." HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \ USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \ OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=1 \ OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \ OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT="$PORT" \ node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run \ --port "$PORT" \ --bind loopback \ --auth token \ --allow-unconfigured \ --ws-log compact HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \ USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \ OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=1 \ OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \ node scripts/run-node.mjs tui \ --session "agent:main:tui-terminal-c663-proof-1779448136227" \ --timeout-ms 180000 HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \ USERPROFILE="$PROOF_HOME" \ OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=1 \ OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \ node --import tsx --input-type=module ``` In the real terminal TUI PTY, I typed and submitted: ```text Live exact-head routing proof from the real terminal TUI. You must use the message tool/action send to send a source reply to this current TUI chat run. Send exactly this text and no extra words, markdown, or quotes: TUI TERMINAL EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448136227. Do not answer with ordinary final text; use the message tool for the visible reply. ``` The separate TUI-mode Gateway proof client connected with `client.id: "openclaw-tui"`, `mode: "ui"`, sent the same message-tool-only prompt through `chat.send`, waited for the live `chat` final event, and queried `chat.history` over the Gateway WebSocket. Evidence after fix: The actual terminal TUI rendered this standalone assistant reply after the typed prompt: ```text TUI TERMINAL EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448136227 ``` The terminal TUI `chat.history` verifier returned: ```json { "ok": true, "head": "c663d2ab000a54c8263b53d3990a454ce9e8fa4c", "terminalTui": true, "sessionKey": "agent:main:tui-terminal-c663-proof-1779448136227", "staleRouteBefore": { "lastChannel": "telegram", "lastTo": "", "deliveryContext": { "channel": "telegram", "to": "" } }, "terminalRenderedText": "TUI TERMINAL EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448136227", "historyMessageCount": 4, "historyHasAssistantSourceReplyText": true, "sourceReplyToolResult": { "status": "ok", "deliveryStatus": "sent", "channel": "webchat", "target": "current-run", "sourceReplyDeliveryMode": "message_tool_only", "sourceReplySink": "internal-ui", "sourceReplyText": "TUI TERMINAL EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448136227", "message": "TUI TERMINAL EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448136227", "dryRun": false } } ``` The TUI-mode Gateway proof returned: ```json { "ok": true, "head": "c663d2ab000a54c8263b53d3990a454ce9e8fa4c", "client": { "id": "openclaw-tui", "mode": "ui" }, "sessionKey": "agent:main:tui-c663-live-proof-1779448039395", "staleRouteBefore": { "lastChannel": "telegram", "lastTo": "", "deliveryContext": { "channel": "telegram", "to": "" } }, "liveFinalRunId": "idem-tui-c663-1779448039395:internal-source-reply:0", "liveFinalText": "TUI EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448039395", "liveFinalWasSourceReply": true, "historyMessageCount": 4, "historyHasAssistantSourceReplyText": true, "sourceReplyToolResult": { "status": "ok", "deliveryStatus": "sent", "channel": "webchat", "target": "current-run", "sourceReplyDeliveryMode": "message_tool_only", "sourceReplySink": "internal-ui", "sourceReplyText": "TUI EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448039395", "message": "TUI EXACT HEAD C663 SOURCE REPLY 1779448039395", "dryRun": false }, "eventCount": 21, "runtimeMs": 15421 } ``` Observed result after fix: The message-tool source reply was visible in the active terminal TUI on exact head `c663d2ab000a54c8263b53d3990a454ce9e8fa4c`, visible as a live `chat` final to a TUI-mode Gateway client with run id `idem-tui-c663-1779448039395:internal-source-reply:0`, persisted into `chat.history`, and resolved to `target: "current-run"` / `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"` despite the stale Telegram route and skipped external channels. The tool result retained the compatibility channel `webchat`, so existing consumers of the old message-tool result shape do not lose that field. What was not tested: Live Telegram/Slack/Discord delivery was not rerun because this PR does not change their external delivery paths; the proof intentionally skipped channels so a stale Telegram fallback could not be the reason the TUI saw the reply. Control UI static assets were disabled in the proof config to avoid unrelated local UI build work. The throwaway proof token and temp proof home were not committed. Before evidence: Earlier base negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834` reproduced the failure shape: the model used the `message` tool and the source reply was persisted with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`, but the active TUI returned to idle without rendering the standalone reply. The maintainer-added screenshot below also captures the reported user-visible failure: the assistant said it resent the summary, then acknowledged it had gone to the webchat surface rather than the TUI. ## Verification - `git diff --check` - `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|mjs)$' | xargs node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` (0 errors on 24 files) - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/utils/message-channel.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` (13 files, 572 tests passed) - `codex review --base origin/main` (clean; no actionable findings) - Exact-head live proof on `c663d2ab000a54c8263b53d3990a454ce9e8fa4c`: fresh isolated Gateway + real terminal TUI PTY + TUI-mode Gateway client, both with stale Telegram route and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, described above ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible: yes - Config/env changes: no - Public SDK/API changes: no - External channel behavior changes: no - Migration needed: no
target.author: rubencu
